title: 'This is not our war': Lebanese people voice anger against Hezbollah
subhead: Displaced by Israeli bombings in southern Lebanon and Beirut's southern suburbs, tens of thousands of Lebanese people, mainly Shiites, have sought refuge in Christian neighborhoods, fueling fears of further strikes there.

title: Trump says war on Iran will end 'very soon,' calls conflict 'very complete'
subhead: The comments helped lift markets and ease energy fears even as details on any ceasefire remain vague.

title: Israel's double-edged ambitions in Lebanon
subhead: The Israeli army is seeking to complete the destructive work it began during the autumn 2024 war and to bring Hezbollah to its knees. Yet its airstrikes, which have caused numerous civilian casualties, could paradoxically restore legitimacy to the Shiite militia and plunge Lebanon into lasting chaos.

title: 'The new Iran will have a crucial asset: Its diaspora'
subhead: Researchers Mohammad-Reza Djalili and Thierry Kellner argue that, despite deep ideological divisions, Iranians living abroad are poised to play a decisive role in shaping the regime that could emerge in the aftermath of the current conflict.

title: 2026 French municipal elections: Has environmentalism become less divisive at the local level?
subhead: While environmental ambitions have waned nationally in France, emblematic measures such as greening public spaces or insulating buildings have become more widely accepted at the municipal level. However, some issues remain contentious.

One week before France's local elections, electorate leans right, poll shows
subhead: With environmental issues taking a back seat, security issues coming to the fore and a clear increase in support for the far-right Rassemblement National, the survey conducted by Ipsos BVA for Le Monde, Cevipof and the Jean Jaurès Foundation paints an unprecedented picture of the French political landscape.

title: War in Iran: Europe's complicit silence on international law
subhead: European reluctance to condemn the Israeli-American intervention in Iran flies in the face of the continent's values. No one can believe that Benjamin Netanyahu and Donald Trump's goal is to defend the interests of the Iranian people.

title: France's Iranian community torn between republicans and monarchists
subhead: Many Iranians in France hope for regime change back home. However, those in favor of democracy are losing ground to supporters of a return to the monarchy, led by the son of Iran's former shah.
